content: When you send a message on the internet, it’s important to keep it safe so only the person you want can read it. Encryption is like turning your message into a secret code that only the person with the special key can understand. Imagine you write a message, and then a magic machine scrambles all the letters so no one else can read it. The person you send the message to has a special key that can unlock the scrambled message and turn it back into words. The keys are kept very safe and only shared with the people who should read the message, so others can’t unlock it. The keys are usually stored in secret places on computers or phones, and there are strong rules to protect them. Sometimes hackers try, but encryption makes it super hard for them to read your messages without the key. Encryption can keep all kinds of information safe, like pictures, videos, and even phone calls.
